differential_diagnoses: Ectopic variceal bleeding secondary to portal biliopathy and portal hypertension
Biliary-vascular fistula (hemobilia) related to previous hepaticojejunostomy
Portal cavernoma with localized mesenteric venous hypertension
Angiodysplasia in the setting of portal hypertensive gastropathy/enteropathy
Dieulafoy lesion of the small bowel
next_steps: Balloon-assisted enteroscopy targeted at the hepaticojejunostomy site to evaluate for ectopic varices
Selective visceral angiography (celiac and superior mesenteric arteries) to identify occult vascular sources or fistulae
Percutaneous transhepatic portal venography and manometry to assess portal pressure and map collateral circulation
Interventional radiology-guided fine-needle aspiration or biopsy of the perihilar soft-tissue abnormality
Multidisciplinary review involving interventional radiology, hepatobiliary surgery, and advanced endoscopy
